repo_prior_art.cite: eid_lnsem_cumulant_id — retained a minimal-order cumulant question but did not state the order-(2m+2) bivariate direction-separation kernel.

published_prior_art.cite: ChenPengHuangEtAl2025Direction uses a k=m+2 cumulant matrix reaching order 2m+3; SchkodaRobevaDrton2024 nevertheless shows the relevant bivariate LvLiNGAM structural decompositions have compatible source-swaps, so the claimed singleton fiber is not viable.

ChenPengHuangEtAl2025Direction gives rank/determinant direction criteria and ChenHuangCaiEtAl2024 treats one latent variable, neither an arbitrary-m opposite-arrow compatibility ideal with an asserted codimension.

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_lnsem_cumulant_id — warns that a failed cumulant witness is not an information-order lower bound, but supplies no Euclidean-open real reverse-twin construction.

published_prior_art.cite: ChenPengHuangEtAl2025Direction supplies a sufficient rank criterion, not a Euclidean-open moment-feasible opposite-arrow twin family through order 2m+1.
SchkodaRobevaDrton2024 uses cumulant rank conditions and compatible effect sets, not a real semialgebraic atlas of the bivariate opposite-arrow compatibility locus.

structure_flags.label: Theorem 1: truncated separation
structure_flags.one_line: The load-bearing open kernel is labeled a theorem even though it is the proposal's strongest unresolved result; reclassify the repaired headline as a conjecture.

novelty_flags.label: related_work: Genin2021StatisticalUndecidability comparison
novelty_flags.one_line: Genin2021StatisticalUndecidability proves failure of unrestricted finite-sample statistical decidability under latent confounding, but does not identify or characterize this proposal's real feasible locus E_m; the text overattributes the need to restrict away from E_m to that paper.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1: singleton forward/reverse fiber claim
soundness_flags.one_line: The forward map is invariant when S_0=(1,gamma) is exchanged with any latent S_i=(1,rho_i), with their cumulant lists exchanged; thus R^right_m cannot generically be singleton modulo permutations of only 1,...,m (already reflected in SchkodaRobevaDrton2024's compatible source-swap analysis).

accepted_scope_caveats.label: honest_scope: population and lower-order boundary
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: No real lower-order information bound, finite-sample inference, dependent-latent robustness, or direct-effect-coefficient identification is claimed.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Any repaired headline is conditional on a nonempty real moment-feasible generic region and must concern arrow separation only, not unique recovery of the labeled direct-effect source.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace singleton structural recovery by a quotient that also permits exchanging S_0 with every nonvertical latent source, and state only opposite-arrow exclusion for the direction selector.

Recast the corrected main result as a conjecture and add an explicit compatibility corollary, aligned with SchkodaRobevaDrton2024, that records the finite set of direct-effect/source-label alternatives within a fixed arrow.

Give explicit m=1 and m=2 real moment-feasible instances after the quotient repair, and distinguish the m=1 fourth-order orientation claim from ChenHuangCaiEtAl2024's fourth-order edge test and fifth-order mixing-ratio construction.

recommended_next_step: Reject this formulation and redraft around arrow separation modulo all admissible source swaps; then re-run novelty review before Stage 0.
